title: Managing Secrets using kubectl — Verify the Secret
text:
Check that the Secret was created:
kubectl get secrets
The output is similar to:
NAME              TYPE       DATA      AGE
db-user-pass      Opaque     2         51s
View the details of the Secret:
kubectl describe secret db-user-pass
The output is similar to:
Name:            db-user-pass
Namespace:       default
Labels:          <none>
Annotations:     <none>

Type:            Opaque

Data
====
password:    12 bytes
username:    5 bytes
The commands
kubectl get
and
kubectl describe
avoid showing the contents
of a
Secret
by default. This is to protect the
Secret
from being exposed
accidentally, or from being stored in a terminal log.
